title: eremophila decipiens subsp. decipiens
intro: Eremophila decipiens subsp. decipiens, also known as the Western Emu Bush, is a species of flowering plant in the family Scrophulariaceae, native to Western Australia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe and protect the skin. The health benefits of Eremophila decipiens subsp. decipiens include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce inflammation, and protect against infection, making it a popular choice for topical applications. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to support skin health and promote overall well-being.
